title: daucus guttatus subsp. guttatus
intro: Daucus guttatus subsp. guttatus, commonly known as the wild carrot or marsh carrot, is a flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Daucus guttatus subsp. guttatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Daucus guttatus subsp. guttatus is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its roots and leaves can be dried and used in various forms, including capsules and powder.
